name: HMS Kingston Beryl (4.03)

17 Nov 1942 HMS P 614 (Lt. H.W. Wilkinson, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Freetown together with HMS St. Zeno (T/Lt. J.K. Craig, RNVR), HMS Coventry City (T/Lt. J.C. Grose, RNR), another HMS trawler (unable to read the name in the log of HMS P 614) and HMS ML 271. (2)
21 Apr 1943 HMS Usurper (Lt. D.R.O. Mott, DSC, RN) and HMS Satyr (Lt. T.S. Weston, RN) departed Holy Loch for Lerwick. They were escorted by HMS Kingston Beryl (T/Lt. T.C. Kidd, RNVR). (3)
25 Apr 1943 HMS Stubborn (Lt. A.A. Duff, RN) ended her 1st war patrol at Lerwick. She departed later the same day for Holy Loch. She was escorted by HMS Kingston Beryl (T/Lt. T.C. Kidd, RNVR). (4)

29 Jul 1943 At 0317 B/29, in thick fog, in position 278° - South Stack Light - 13.5 nautical miles, HMCS Iroquois (Lt. E.T.G. Madgwick, RCN) and HMS Kingston Beryl (T/Lt. T.C. Kidd, RNVR) collided. The trawler was not damaged but the stem of HMCS Iroquois was broken about 8 feet down from the forecastle deck. HMCS Iroquois was ordered to proceed to Greenock for repairs. She arrived at Greenock late in the afternoon. (5)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
